title: Top CBS producer faces termination as show’s ratings plummet to embarrassingly low numbers
description: CBS sources have revealed that a top producer at ‘CBS Evening News’ faces the chopping block as ratings plummet below four million.  Executive Producer Kim Harvey, who’s helmed the broadcast since last August, could be terminated if she fails to save the show from the abyss.  In January, Tony Dokoupil was chosen by CBS News Editor in Chief Bari Weiss to anchor the show in hopes of reviving the broadcast.  Though Dokoupil’s debut drew 4.4 million viewers, with a high of 4.6 million, viewership declined, reaching as low as 3.8 million, according to Nielsen ratings.  “It’s pretty terrible. Once you’re under 4 million, you’ve got to be worried that you’re in a death spiral,” a CBS insider claimed.  “If they can’t retain an audience in the middle of a war, God help you when the war ends.”

title: NSW government debates funding for businesses affected by Great Western Highway closure
description: The New South Wales government is having active conversations about whether to offer a funding package to small businesses impacted by the partial closure of the Great Western Highway.

title: ‘Pathologically hostile’ Joe Kent thinks biggest ‘exporter of Islamist terrorism’ not a threat
description: National Review Senior Writer Noah Rothman blasts Joe Kent for his “pathological hostility” towards Israel following his resignation as the Director of the US National Counterterrorism Centre.  “If you’re anywhere near national security, you should understand that the Iranian regime always poses an imminent threat to American civilians, soldiers, and its national interests abroad,” Mr Rothman told Sky News Digital Presenter James Bolt.  “Mr Kent alleges that Israel is essentially responsible, not only for this war … but also, it’s responsible for the Syrian civil war, apparently according to this letter … and it’s also responsible for the Iraq war.   “A figure like that should not be at the head of America’s counterterrorism apparatus at a time when we are at war with the world’s foremost exporter of Islamist terrorism.”

title: RBA rate hike driven by ‘out of control’ inflation, not Iran war: Paterson
description: Shadow Defence Minister James Paterson says the RBA’s decision to lift the cash rate to 4.1 per cent was driven by already ‘out of control’ inflation, not the Iran war.

title: CNN analyst stunned as poll shows MAGA 'approve 100 per cent' of Donald Trump
description: CNN data analyst Harry Enten has been left shocked after a new poll found that “100 per cent” of MAGA voters support US President Donald Trump  On Wednesday, Enten analysed Trump’s support base as the president’s ongoing war with Iran enters its third week.  “Sometimes you look at the polling data, and there are numbers that just jump off the screen at you, and this is one of those,” Enten said.  The analyst pointed out that while some Republicans “disapprove” of President Trump, they do not identify as members of the MAGA “movement”  “Now, there are some Republicans who disapprove of Donald John Trump, but they are not members of the Make America Great Again movement,” Enten said.  “The bottom line is this: if you are a member of MAGA, you approve of Donald Trump.”

title: Andrew reportedly says his teddy bears ‘won’t cope’ with Royal Lodge exit
description: Sky News host James Macpherson reacts to reports Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor is having a “full-on meltdown” over being moved out of the Royal Lodge, allegedly claiming his teddy bears “won’t cope with the change.”  “A source close to Andrew says …. ‘it really seems like being forced to move has sent him into a full-on meltdown because he keeps telling people the bears won’t cope with the change’,” Mr Macpherson said.   “He’s complained that he shouldn’t be made to move because ‘this is the bear’s home too’ … forget the Epstein stuff, that’s a reason why he should not be in the succession line to become King.”

title: ‘She’s met her match’: Kamala Harris meets drag impersonator in viral moment
description: Sky News host Danica De Giorgio reacts to a video of former US vice president Kamala Harris meeting her drag queen impersonator.   “Kamala Harris, the word salad queen, guess what? She’s back, and she’s met her own match in drag,” Ms De Giorgio said.  “I can’t tell who’s who at this point; they look exactly the same, and they sound exactly the same.  “So, Donald Trump is out there fighting wars, and Kamala Harris is watching herself as a drag impersonator.”

title: ‘Pick up the phone’: Pauline Hanson demands Albanese 'stand united' with Trump on Iran
description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson claims Prime Minister Anthony Albanese needs to “stand united” with the US to “stamp out” evil throughout the world.  “Hasn’t Trump reached out once before to ask for assistance, and yet we did nothing to assist?” Ms Hanson told Sky News Digital Presenter Gabriella Power.  “When you’re a mate with someone and a close ally, as we are with the AUKUS deal, then you pick up the phone.”

title: Unemployment jumps to 4.3 per cent in surprise spike
description: The unemployment rate has risen from 4.1 per cent to 4.3 per cent, according to the latest data from the Australian Bureau of Statistics.  The increase comes as a surprise to many economists, who expected the rate to hold steady at 4.1 per cent.  Full-time employment fell by 35,000, contributing to the 0.2 percentage point rise in February.

title: Crowds gather as a funeral is held in Iran for Ali Larijani
description: A funeral procession has been held in Tehran's Revolution Square for former security chief Ali Larijani and Basij militia commander Gholamreza Soleimani.  Both men were killed in Israeli air strikes earlier this week.  The crowd also mourned sailors who died on the Iranian Navy ship torpedoed by a US submarine off the coast of Sri Lanka two weeks ago.  Iran's state television broadcast the event.

title: ‘Scallywag gossip’: Jacinta Allan dismisses leadership spill rumours
description: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan calls leadership spill rumours “anonymous gossip” spread by “scallywags”.

title: ‘Out of control’: Pauline Hanson blasts ‘arrogant’ Labor for ‘opening the floodgates’ on migration
description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson blasts the “arrogant” Labor government for opening the migration floodgates.  “This government is so arrogant, they really are, they know how the people feel,” Ms Hanson told Sky News Digital Presenter Gabriella Power.  “They keep opening up the floodgates.”

title: US Federal Reserve holds interest rates
description: The US Federal Reserve has held interest rates but has yet to factor the Middle Eastern conflict into its equations.  The Federal Open Market Committee voted almost unanimously to remain in the 3.5 to 3.75 range.  The rate is projected to fall once by the end of 2026.  Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell outlined the US financial outlook.  Mr Powell added GDP was expected to rise above previous projections, while unemployment was steady.  The rate decision is at odds with US President Donald Trump's demands for a significant rate cut.

title: ‘People want change’: Pauline Hanson unleashes on Labor’s climate change policies
description: One Nation Leader Pauline Hanson claims the Labor government’s climate change policies have had an impact on many Australians.  “All these climate change policies that the government has brought in has had an impact on many Australians,” Ms Hanson told Sky News Digital Presenter Gabriella Power.  “People want change.”

title: Queen Mary and King Frederik arrive in Hobart for last leg of royal tour
description: Queen Mary and King Frederik have arrived in the Queen's hometown of Hobart for the last leg of their royal tour.  The Danish Royals are expected to visit a number of local businesses and organisations.  The state visit marks their first official visit as the reigning monarchs of Denmark.
